semantic.role: Define the automated CI pipeline for the ArchSpine project to ensure code quality, build success, and pre-release validation.
responsibilities: Verification of code on push to main and pull requests across multiple Node.js versions (20 and 22).
Running lint, build, test, and protocol asset validation.
Executing a release gate for comprehensive pre-publishing checks.
Performing a package smoke check to verify npm packaging integrity.
outOfScope: Deployment or publishing steps to registry or production environments.
Manual triggers or environment-specific configurations beyond Node 20/22.

English.purpose: This workflow defines the Continuous Integration pipeline for the ArchSpine project. It automates code verification, builds, tests, documentation generation, and pre-release validation to maintain high code quality and prevent regressions.
English.context_and_audience: The document is intended for project maintainers, contributors, and anyone reviewing the CI setup. It explains what checks run automatically on every push to main and on pull requests, ensuring that all proposed changes meet the project's quality standards before merging.
key_takeaways: CI automatically runs lint, build, test, and asset validation on Node 20 and 22 for every push and pull request.
A dedicated release gate job runs additional checks before any potential release.
Documentation builds and package smoke checks are part of the pipeline to catch issues early.
The workflow is designed with cancellation of in-progress runs for efficiency.
